rationale: Both correctly diagnose the run-level/dummy-weight root cause matching the thread's 'invalid' resolution and cite specific test-helper files/functions. Y goes further: it explains the crucial nuance that pytest.mark.full_model is a selection marker, not a run-level setter (clarifying why node-ID selection didn't prevent the bug), addresses both the text ('Joe') and audio (Welsh noise) symptoms, lists concrete prerequisites (real weights, GPU memory split, video path), and adds a reasonable forward-looking suggestion (warn/skip guard) without contradicting the closed-as-invalid outcome. X's answer is also correct and grounded but slightly less complete and appears truncated at the end.
